name: Sinus Arrhythmia
identifyBy: Irregular rhythm with normal P waves preceding every QRS
Rate increases with inspiration, decreases with expiration
All P waves identical morphology - confirms sinus origin
Common in children, young adults, athletes - generally benign
management: No intervention required - benign normal variant
Common in young, fit individuals and athletes
pearls: Sinus arrhythmia is a sign of good autonomic tone - more pronounced in athletes and children
Distinguished from AF by identical P wave morphology preceding every QRS
Non-respiratory sinus arrhythmia (unrelated to breathing) may indicate sinus node disease - investigate if symptomatic
Pronounced in bradycardic patients - may look irregular at rest
differentials: Atrial fibrillation (no P waves, irregularly irregular)
Wandering atrial pacemaker (P wave morphology varies)
Blocked PACs (P waves visible but no QRS)

qaNotes: Sinus rhythm family: P before every QRS unless sinus-node dysfunction pause pattern is specified.
